No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description or objectives links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course material suggests any connection to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content on civil procedure does not relate to health, well-being, or medical services.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides quality tertiary legal education (Target 4.3) and develops professional skills for resolving civil disputes (Target 4.4).

Course Name: Law of Civil Procedure (II)

Gender Equality.reason: There is no mention of gender-specific issues, discrimination, or empowerment in the course syllabus.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course on civil procedure has no relevance to water, sanitation, or environmental health.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No connection to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency is found in the course content.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Civil procedure provides the institutional framework for contract enforcement and protecting labor rights (Target 8.8), indirectly supporting economic activity.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course does not address industrial development, innovation, or infrastructure directly or indirectly.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Civil procedure law is a mechanism to ensure equal opportunity and access to legal remedy (Target 10.3), reducing inequalities of outcome before the law.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence links the course content to urban planning, sustainable communities, or related issues.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course does not cover topics related to consumption patterns, resource efficiency, or waste management.

Climate Action.reason: There is no indication that the course addresses climate change, mitigation, or adaptation strategies.

Life Below Water.reason: The course content has no relevance to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or aquatic life.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence suggests a connection between the course and terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course directly addresses the mechanics of the civil justice system, fundamentally promoting the rule of law and ensuring equal access to justice (Target 16.3).
